behavior_summary: U8P_FIRESHRO implements a simple inspect/U8P_FIRESHRO::look handler and two U8P_FIRESHRO::hit-handling routines for a "fire shroom" item.
U8P_FIRESHRO::look emits a short bark/description "fire shroom" when the player examines the object (process-type U8P_FIRESHRO::look).
Both U8P_FIRESHRO::gotHit and U8P_FIRESHRO::hit implement U8P_FIRESHRO::hit/impact handling: they check an item-level Q flag (via Item::getQ) and set it on first U8P_FIRESHRO::hit (Item::setQ(0x01)).

When a U8P_FIRESHRO::hit source parameter is provided (param1), those routines copy it into temporaries (local1/local2), test whether it refers to an NPC (Item::isNpc) and whether that NPC is dead (Npc::isDead), and then delegate to a shared handler METHOD::func0676 to continue processing/effects.
The Q flag acts as a guard to ensure first-U8P_FIRESHRO::hit activation logic runs only once (prevents repeated activation).
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIRESHRO::look: single linear flow — bark "fire shroom" then suspend/return.
U8P_FIRESHRO::gotHit and U8P_FIRESHRO::hit: conditional gate on Item::getQ(this). If Q is not set, call Item::setQ(0x01,this) to mark activation.

Both U8P_FIRESHRO::hit routines conditionally run additional logic only when a U8P_FIRESHRO::hit-source parameter is present: copy parameter to locals, branch on Item::isNpc(local1), then (if NPC) call Npc::isDead(local2) to decide behavior before invoking METHOD::func0676.
Shared continuation is performed by an external routine METHOD::func0676 rather than inline — U8P_FIRESHRO::hit routines largely prepare state and delegate.
open_questions: What exact gameplay effect METHOD::func0676 applies after the U8P_FIRESHRO::hit preparation (damage, status, item transformation, or audio/visual effect)? (inferred: applies effect; confidence: low)
Does the Item-level Q flag represent a one-time activation, a visual-state toggle, or a persistent quest/consumption marker beyond this item instance? (confidence: medium)
Are there any differences in usage or timing between U8P_FIRESHRO::gotHit and U8P_FIRESHRO::hit beyond naming (they appear to perform the same logic in the excerpt)? (confidence: low)
What is the intended behavior when Npc::isDead(local2) returns true — do the U8P_FIRESHRO::hit routines abort, skip effects, or merely note the state? (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspect/examine description for the fire shroom item (player-facing bark).
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player examines/looks at the item (process-type 0x0208).
May also be called when the engine needs a short object label/description.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::look
function_map.role: Provide short examine/U8P_FIRESHRO::look description for the item (bark: "fire shroom").

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle an incoming U8P_FIRESHRO::hit/impact: ensure item Q flag set, inspect U8P_FIRESHRO::hit-source param, check NPC/death, delegate to METHOD::func0676.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHRO::hit
function_map.role: Handle explicit U8P_FIRESHRO::hit event similar to U8P_FIRESHRO::gotHit: set Q flag if needed, prepare/inspect source param, and call METHOD::func0676.

external_calls.purpose: Query the item-level Q flag to determine if first-U8P_FIRESHRO::hit activation is needed.
Set the item-level Q flag (0x01) to mark the item as activated/processed on first U8P_FIRESHRO::hit.
Test whether the provided U8P_FIRESHRO::hit-source parameter refers to an NPC before running NPC-specific checks.
Check whether the referenced NPC (U8P_FIRESHRO::hit source) is dead; used to influence subsequent handling.
Delegate to shared U8P_FIRESHRO::hit-processing routine to apply effects/damage/continuation logic after initial checks.

reads.name: Item.Q
reads.likely_effect: Used as a per-item guard to detect whether first-U8P_FIRESHRO::hit activation/processing has already occurred (read by Item::getQ).

writes.name: Item.Q
writes.likely_effect: Set to 0x01 on first U8P_FIRESHRO::hit via Item::setQ to mark the item as activated/processed and prevent re-running initial U8P_FIRESHRO::hit logic.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_FIRESHRO implements a simple inspect/U8P_FIRESHRO::look handler and two U8P_FIRESHRO::hit-handling routines for a "fire shroom" item.
